frameworks.name: latent structure learning
frameworks.one_line: Infers unobserved social group categories beyond dyadic similarity.

frameworks.name: computational model-based fMRI
frameworks.one_line: Trial-by-trial model estimates regressed against neural signal.

frameworks.name: dyadic similarity heuristic
frameworks.one_line: Pairwise similarity treated as alternative basis for allyship.

core_question: How does the brain learn and represent latent social group boundaries beyond explicit categories and dyadic similarity?
key_assumptions: Allyship estimates can be modeled trial-by-trial from behavior
Latent group structure and dyadic similarity are dissociable computations
BOLD variability tracks distinct algorithmic signals
manipulations: Two competing allyship signals (dyadic vs latent-structure) as model regressors
Trial-by-trial agent-participant interactions during ally choice
key_findings: Dyadic-similarity allyship tracks mPFC/pregenual ACC activity
Latent-structure allyship tracks right anterior insula activity
rAI variability predicts ally-choice behavior; pgACC variability does not
interpretations: Distinct neural substrates support similarity-based vs latent-group social inference
rAI mediates the computation distinguishing 'us' from 'them'
